symptom: TDO always returns 0x00000000, IDCODE reads as 0x00000000
root_cause: CPLD version 0xFFFE — TDO path through CPLD is dead, cable cannot read back
lesson: If IDCODE is all-zeros, check CPLD version first — it's a hardware path issue, not a software bug

symptom: Loopback mode allows testing serial port without FPGA
root_cause: FT232RL can loop TX to RX internally when physically shorted
lesson: Loopback mode provides software test capability independent of hardware FPGA connection. Useful for verifying serial port functionality when FPGA is unavailable.

data.instruction: Short TX to RX with wire (pin 4 -> pin 5 on DB9)

action: UART echo test with FT232RL JTAG cable
symptom: FT232RL JTAG cable cannot do UART - pins wired for JTAG (TCK/TDI/TDO/TMS), not UART (TX/RX)
root_cause: FT232RL JTAG cable (PID 0x6001) uses MPSSE mode for JTAG. Physical pins are TDI/TDO/TMS/TCK, not TXD/RXD. Loopback test fails even without FPGA.
lesson: USB-to-JTAG cable cannot be used as USB-to-UART. Need dedicated UART adapter (CP2102/CH340/FT232R-UART) or use FPGA's built-in JTAG-UART bridge.

symptom: Cannot execute UART echo test plan - hardware blockers prevent all tests
root_cause: 1) CPLD 0xFFFE causes TDO stuck at 0, JTAG cannot read IDCODE. 2) /dev/cu.usbserial-2140 is JTAG-only, cannot do UART. Plan assumes working JTAG + UART capability.
lesson: Check hardware compatibility before writing test plans. FT232RL JTAG (PID 0x6001) != FT232R-UART. CPLD 0xFFFE is a hard blocker for all JTAG operations.

data.step1_build: PASS - Fixed unused constants, binary created
data.step2_loopback: BLOCKED - JTAG cable has no TX/RX pins

action: DSLogic U2basic Integration — full signal analysis infrastructure
symptom: DSLogic U2basic integration complete with bilingual documentation, channel mapping JSON, DSView presets, and Zig diagnostic tool
root_cause: Implemented Phase 1-2 of DSLogic U2basic integration plan. Physical connection guide, channel mappings, presets, and CLI tool created.
lesson: DSLogic U2basic provides 16-channel logic analysis at 400 MS/s with DSView software. Integration includes: (1) Bilingual connection guide in Russian+English, (2) Machine-readable channel mapping JSON, (3) DSView configuration presets for all test workflows, (4) Zig diagnostic tool for DSLogic control and analysis, (5) Updated hardware_state.json with DSLogic section.

data.note: Phase 3 (diagnostic workflows) and Phase 4 (CLI integration) complete. Ready for Phase 5 (pin mapping verification via actual DSLogic captures).

action: DSLogic U2basic USB detection and DSView path verification
symptom: DSLogic device detected via system_profiler, DSView application found in /Applications/DreamSourceLab/DSView
root_cause: DSLogic U2basic is visible in macOS USB system as DreamSourceLab vendor (VID:0x0212, Location:0x02120000/3). DSView v1.3.2 application is installed and available.
lesson: DSLogic U2basic detected successfully. DSView can be launched via /Applications/DreamSourceLab/DSView. Channel mapping and presets are ready for DSView import.

action: Flash bitstream via xc3sprog on Mac (discovery working procedure)
symptom: fxload after replug switches DLC10 PID to 0x0008 (JTAG mode). xc3sprog detects FPGA XC7A100T and flashes 30.6M bitstream successfully in 23.3 seconds.
root_cause: openFPGALoader does NOT support Xilinx DLC10 cable. xc3sprog DOES support xpc cable (VID:PID 0x03fd:0x0008). Key discovery: replug cable physically after first fxload, then run fxload AGAIN.
lesson: JTAG procedure for Mac: (1) fxload → (2) physical replug → (3) fxload again (ensures PID 0x0008) → (4) xc3sprog -c xpc detect → (5) xc3sprog -c xpc <bitstream.bit>

action: Complete Mac JTAG flashing procedure discovery - fxload + replug + fxload + xc3sprog
symptom: Discovery: fxload twice with physical replug between switches DLC10 PID from 0x0013 to 0x0008. xc3sprog (not openFPGALoader) supports xpc cable. FPGA XC7A100T detected and flashed successfully.
root_cause: 1) openFPGALoader does NOT support Xilinx DLC10 cable (VID:PID 0x03fd:xxxx). 2) fxload needs physical replug - second call after replug is CRITICAL for PID switch to 0x0008. 3) xc3sprog has native xpc cable support for 0x03fd:0x0008.
lesson: Mac JTAG Procedure: (1) fxload → (2) PHYSICAL REPLUG DLC10 → (3) fxload again (CRITICAL!) → (4) Verify PID 0x0008 → (5) xc3sprog -c xpc <bitstream.bit>. Never use openFPGALoader for Xilinx DLC10. Script: fpga/tools/mac_jtag_procedure_v2.sh

data.cable: Xilinx DLC10 Platform Cable USB II (clone)
data.key_insight: Physical replug between fxload calls is CRITICAL for PID switch
data.tool_working: xc3sprog (openFPGALoader does NOT support Xilinx DLC10)
data.uart_test: screen terminates immediately - UART echo needs further verification
data.blocker_resolved: Mac JTAG flashing - was BLOCKED, now WORKING

action: UART pin mapping investigation - DSLogic guide vs blink_test.v comments
symptom: DSLogic connection guide shows K20/L20 pins measured on PCB. blink_test.v comments mention E21/F21 pins. uart_bridge_fixed.xdc had K20/L20 (correct per DSLogic measurements). uart_bridge_fixed.bit exists but was never reflashed after pin corrections.
root_cause: Pin mapping confusion - DSLogic guide based on actual PCB measurements is authoritative. blink_test.v comments may be for different setup or incorrect. uart_bridge_fixed.v and uart_bridge_fixed.xdc had mismatched comments.
lesson: Trust physical measurements over comments. When in doubt, verify with multimeter or probe on actual board. uart_bridge_fixed.bit (created 10:27) predates pin fixes - needs reflash.

data.next_step: Flash uart_bridge_fixed.bit and test UART echo
